title: aquilegia formosa subsp. truncata
intro: Aquilegia formosa subsp. truncata, also known as the Western red columbine, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the buttercup family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and inflammation, due to its antispasmodic and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Aquilegia formosa subsp. truncata include reducing symptoms of bronchitis, coughs, and colds, as well as acting as a natural anti-inflammatory agent. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
